items.title: Review & Preview: The Rally Stumbles
items.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell took the steam out of the market’s rally, with equities closing lower on Tuesday.  The S&P 500 was off 0.6%, the Dow Jones Industrial Average shed 0.2%, and the Nasdaq Composite fell nearly 1%.  Tech stocks were dragging on the Nasdaq earlier in the day as Wall Street took some profit on AI stocks and bought up a few laggards, including value, small-caps, and dividend stocks.

items.title: Micron, Bigbear.Ai, Lithium Americas, Virgin Galactic And Amazon: Why These 5 Stocks Are On Investors' Radars Today
items.description: The S&P 500 fell 0.55% to 6,656.92, the Nasdaq declined 0.95% to 22,573.47.

items.title: US stocks retreat from records as tech giants fall
items.description: Wall Street's bull run showed signs of fatigue Tuesday as major indices retreated from records on drops by Amazon, Nvidia and other tech giants.All three major US indices have finished at records the last three days.

items.title: Stocks to Watch Recap: TSMC, Boeing, Kenvue, AutoZone
items.description: ↗️ Boeing (BA): The plane maker said Uzbekistan Airways would buy 14 787-9 Dreamliner jets, with options for eight more. The deal is worth over $8 billion, according to President Trump. Boeing shares rose 2% Tuesday.

items.title: Tech Stocks Drag Indexes Lower, Snapping Winning Streak
items.description: Major U.S. benchmarks slipped from record levels, while Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell left the door open for more rate cuts.
